pains.summary: Last-touch attribution fails to show true value of non-converting channels like LinkedIn and events
pains.quote: LinkedIn is super tough to assign that value to, right? Because it's a touch, right? And so oftentimes, CFOs in particular cut investment into LinkedIn because it's an influencer, not necessarily a converting channel.
pains.speaker: Adrianna Burrows

pains.summary: No ability to predict campaign performance or make in-flight adjustments
pains.quote: I cannot answer at the highest level, not only combining the first and the last, if I had a dollar, what's performing, you know, and it, the quarter. It always has to be a post-mortem view when it's too late to impact it.
pains.speaker: Adrianna Burrows

pains.summary: Orphaned opportunities with no campaign attribution due to contact role changes
pains.quote: So I handed over to Shahan and Shahan becomes the primary contact on the opportunity. Shahan has absolutely no campaigns associated with him whatsoever, ever. I'm now removed from the opportunity. The campaign was associated with me.
pains.speaker: Jonathan Parramore

pains.summary: Heuristic pipeline coverage multipliers are inadequate for different segments
pains.quote: we throw out pipeline coverage model on numbers, multipliers that I think are just heuristics, you know, nothing more. So I think there's some very interesting things there as we go to segments.
pains.speaker: Kael Kelly

pains.summary: No unified view of customer journey across marketing, SDR, and sales touches
pains.quote: we're a little bit blind to outreach to you know, exactly how many are in sequence that we're also distributed by this campaign, whatever. It's like putting all that together gets quite difficult.
pains.speaker: Jonathan Parramore

pains.summary: Manual, code-heavy reporting in Snowflake with no visualization or drag-and-drop capability
pains.quote: we are doing a lot of that in Snowflake today. It's very complex for us. So, you know, we don't have the capability to like visualize all of this or drag and drop or report out on it easily. It's like hundreds and hundreds of lines of code.
pains.speaker: Theo Stewart

pains.summary: Inability to track net new vs existing customer activity in marketing channels
pains.quote: because we have a lot of existing customers and partners using our website and touching our different marketing channels, sometimes it's hard to get a clear signal around like what is actually net new and how that's progressing through our Funnel.
pains.speaker: Theo Stewart

pains.summary: Inquiries in Salesforce don't connect straight through to opportunity - one person on account acquired but another ends up being the point
pains.quote: sometimes our inquiries in salesforce don't connect straight through to the opportunity one person on an account acquired but another one ends up being the point
pains.speaker: Anthony Sharot

pains.summary: No organizational understanding of what RevSure does - people hoping it does everything on the planet
pains.quote: most people in Avalara don't know what is and isn't in scope of its ability they're probably just hoping it does everything on the planet
pains.speaker: Anthony Sharot

pains.summary: Broken guidance and fragmented ownership led to inability to make streamlined decisions
pains.quote: he reaches out to GAI or Martech, all of these guys were looped in to provide guidance. So guidance was a little broken, to be very honest with you, for us to be able to make streamlined decisions.
pains.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

pains.summary: Salesforce data going through Snowflake creates multiple validation layers and complexity
pains.quote: All of this is coming from Snowflake, so there is a couple of layers for us to make sure the Salesforce data is reflected correctly in Snowflake, and in return, reflected correctly
pains.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

pains.summary: Attribution model lacks sales touchpoints, creating incomplete customer journey story
pains.quote: To me, that's a very incomplete journey right now. That's a very incomplete story that we're going to tell to the leaders. We're literally regarding the sales
pains.speaker: Ramin Amin

pains.summary: Manual process required to update funnel logic when definitions change
pains.quote: if there are changes to logic then we'll have to like recreate the final definitions and I think I want to make
pains.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

pains.summary: Marketing spend data definition unclear and scattered across spreadsheets
pains.quote: Finance track in Excel has to reach out to certain marketing channel owners and event owners and things like that. So it's everywhere in Excel.
pains.speaker: Juyeon Yun

pains.summary: Opportunity history gets overwritten in Salesforce, making attribution validation difficult
pains.quote: Salesforce is just like a snapshot and it just overwrites the history that I'm not really even sure if Avalara, like, has that information.
pains.speaker: Theo Stewart

tools_switched_from.reason: Vista recommended RevSure as better AI-first solution across portfolio companies; Marketo Measure was erroneously contracted
tools_switched_from.quote: Previously bringing on Marketo measure tool, which already been paid for. And so, you know, if we end up not implementing a system like Rapture, then we go back to implementing a system like Marketo
tools_switched_from.speaker: John Philip McCoy

tools_switched_from.reason: Too manual, requires hundreds of lines of code, lacks predictive capability
tools_switched_from.quote: we are doing a lot of that in Snowflake today. It's very complex for us. So, you know, we don't have the capability to like visualize all of this or drag and drop or report out on it easily. It's like hundreds and hundreds of lines of code.
tools_switched_from.speaker: Theo Stewart

tools_switched_from.reason: Already have insight in Adobe Analytics and 6sense, trying to determine if RevSure adds value beyond these existing tools
tools_switched_from.quote: we have that insight already in Adobe Analytics, I believe, as well as Native and Sixth Sense. So I'm trying to work out, is this a big win or not really worth paying for?
tools_switched_from.speaker: Anthony Sharot

tools_switched_from.reason: Limited to last-touch attribution, no multi-touch visibility into customer journey
tools_switched_from.quote: in our current views in our funnel and regatta dashboard and everything funnel metrics emails that contribution is like so little so um and then journey
tools_switched_from.speaker: Juyeon Yun

tools_switched_from.reason: Cannot build comprehensive MTA dashboard, too much work to replicate RevSure capabilities
tools_switched_from.quote: it doesn't make sense for us to build an entire dashboard in Power BI. It's just too much work.
tools_switched_from.speaker: Juyeon Yun

political_tussles.summary: CFOs cut LinkedIn spend because last-touch attribution makes it look non-converting
political_tussles.quote: oftentimes, CFOs in particular cut investment into LinkedIn because it's an influencer, not necessarily a converting channel.
political_tussles.speaker: Adrianna Burrows

political_tussles.summary: Marketing cannot see if SDRs are actioning leads or where distribution breaks down
political_tussles.quote: once it's an outreach, we're a little bit blind to outreach to you know, exactly how many are in sequence that we're also distributed by this campaign, whatever. It's like putting all that together gets quite difficult.
political_tussles.speaker: Jonathan Parramore

political_tussles.summary: Marketing wants tool but Jonathan insists IT must dedicate sprints; fears under-resourcing will cause failure like past tools
political_tussles.quote: I will call Scott right now and tell him to buy this if everybody commits, like, here's my two head count, and Shathan says, here's my six to 12 sprints that you have over the next quarter with fully resourced engineering teams to make this work.
political_tussles.speaker: Jonathan Parramore

political_tussles.summary: Marketing questions if RevSure's data transformation conflicts with centralized Snowflake analytics team
political_tussles.quote: when I hear transformation, I'm, I just sort of wonder does this become at odds with what this, the analytic, it's not, this is not a team that lives in marketing, it's a, it's a centralized service. How, how does that, how do, how do those two sort of play together and mesh and so forth?
political_tussles.speaker: SEA-5FLR-Puyallup

political_tussles.summary: Sales concerned that marketing gets undue credit for opportunities that sales worked through calls and emails
political_tussles.quote: i made five calls 10 email they responded back blah then eventually you're giving all the credits to marketing why because salesforce and page views are the ones that you're capturing
political_tussles.speaker: Ramin Amin

political_tussles.summary: Disagreement over whether to do limited pilot or full marketing rollout with limited validated use cases
political_tussles.quote: i have a feeling that kale was like oh is that really necessary i think we should roll it out to entire marketing i have a gut feeling that kale might say this one
political_tussles.speaker: Juyeon Yun

contrarians.claim: Don't need AI for basic campaign member tracking; need AI for gaps in data capture
contrarians.why_unusual: Typically vendors pitch AI for sophisticated attribution; Avalara inverts this saying basic tracking is solvable, the hard problem is data infrastructure
contrarians.quote: You might say well we can just use the campaign members well we can do that today like that's not a super difficult thing we don't need AI for that.
contrarians.speaker: Jonathan Parramore

contrarians.claim: Every tool at Avalara fails because of under-resourcing, not tool quality
contrarians.why_unusual: Blunt institutional self-criticism rare in vendor evaluations; usually buyers blame vendors
contrarians.quote: Uh, Gainsight is a great tool. We under-resourced it and at Avalare it sucks. Outreach is a great tool. At Avalara, we under-resourced it, and it sucks. I mean, I could just name off every market-leading technology that's ever existed. We have bought, and at Avalara, we under-resourced it, and it sucks.
contrarians.speaker: Jonathan Parramore

contrarians.claim: Willing to pay for 6sense de-anonymization credits even though they already have similar insight in Adobe Analytics and 6sense
contrarians.why_unusual: Most companies would not pay for duplicate account identification capabilities across three platforms; shows unusual willingness to invest in redundant data sources
contrarians.quote: we have that insight already in Adobe Analytics, I believe, as well as Native and Sixth Sense. So I'm trying to work out, is this a big win or not really worth paying for?
contrarians.speaker: Anthony Sharot

contrarians.claim: All activities should NOT be automatically considered attribution touchpoints - need filters
contrarians.why_unusual: Most MTA vendors advocate for capturing all touchpoints; Avalara pushes back on blanket inclusion of sales activities
contrarians.quote: we cannot just blanket say that all activities are touch points uh can you send the link to this journey as well
contrarians.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

contrarians.claim: Hidden Markov Model (not standard attribution) chosen specifically because it's 'easy to interpret' with built-in time decay
contrarians.why_unusual: Most sophisticated marketing teams avoid rule-based models; choosing HMM for interpretability over accuracy is counter to typical vendor recommendations
contrarians.quote: my current boat is definitely using hidden markup chain mainly because two reasons one is easy to interpret and then two is it does have time decay already built into it
contrarians.speaker: Juyeon Yun

revsure_problems.problem: No Adobe Analytics integration yet (on roadmap); requires workaround via Snowflake
revsure_problems.quote: You're right, we don't have an Adobe Analytics integration as of now, but that is definitely on our roadmap, number one. And in the time being, like you mentioned, we can always import data, you know, if you're already sending it somewhere, whether into a data lake environment or into like a Salesforce or your marketing automation system, et cetera.
revsure_problems.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

revsure_problems.problem: No Allocadia integration yet; would need to build
revsure_problems.quote: We currently, but we are happy to, if you have that budget, I think we are, you know, we are happy to kind of build that integration. That should not be a problem for us.
revsure_problems.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

revsure_problems.problem: Daily sync only, not real-time; misses rapid Salesforce field changes
revsure_problems.quote: do you snapshot at extremely quick intervals changes that happen in our system? And we were told, no, it's daily. So we are beholden to our own snapshotting and tracking capabilities.
revsure_problems.speaker: Jonathan Parramore

revsure_problems.problem: No pilot/POC possible without full contract and implementation
revsure_problems.quote: unfortunately we can't do that. I know it kind of seems a bit abrupt to respond like that, but we take a lot of pride in our platform. We are highly configurable.
revsure_problems.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

revsure_problems.problem: No multi-account picker for bulk ad account selection - 'it's a shame this can't be done in bulk'
revsure_problems.quote: it's a shame this can't be done in bulk where we could just select you know all of the us all of the gbp accounts from google
revsure_problems.speaker: Anthony Sharot

revsure_problems.problem: Confusion about whether RevSure is full media mix modeling or lower funnel focused - 'not really full media mix modeling at all'
revsure_problems.quote: so it's not really full um media mix modeling at all. It's more focused on the lower funnel, essentially.
revsure_problems.speaker: Anthony Sharot

revsure_problems.problem: Data validation issues - campaign names only align about half the time between RevSure and internal systems
revsure_problems.quote: the campaign names only align, like, about half of the time. So it's like the RevSure platform is, you know, it's using logic to pick a name that we have that we've provided you. but it's a different logic and a different name than what we're using internally
revsure_problems.speaker: Theo Stewart

revsure_problems.problem: Partner referral attribution not matching Avalara's campaign-based last touch model
revsure_problems.quote: there seems to be the biggest issue with this is around partner referrals because rapture is seeing other campaign or other touches at the you know person level whereas avalara is do some work to see if they can't mirror that campaign-based logic
revsure_problems.speaker: Theo Stewart

revsure_problems.problem: Stage zero pipeline data not available in widget, requires engineering work
revsure_problems.quote: we couldn't bring the stage zero here uh in this widget so this is a widget um and uh we
revsure_problems.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

revsure_problems.problem: Client confusion about whether campaign member status update time equals engagement time
revsure_problems.quote: why do I see this as a member status updated is the time that normally engage with the campaign. Is that correct?
revsure_problems.speaker: Ramin Amin

revsure_problems.problem: Too many filter options in dashboard confusing for executive stakeholders
revsure_problems.quote: i'm sure leaders are too lazy if they click on that filter they will send the slack message right away to us what is this which one do we need to even if we like tell them okay this is the instruction when you click on filter you will see like 20 different options
revsure_problems.speaker: Ramin Amin

best_about_revsure.summary: Seeing everything in one place instead of fragmented tools
best_about_revsure.quote: I love seeing everything in one place. Like this tool is, I mean, you know, we're trying to do bits and pieces of this ourselves with, you know, a lot of heavy lifting and so it's really nice to see everything in one clear, you know, easily understandable view. So that a huge amount of value there.
best_about_revsure.speaker: Theo Stewart

best_about_revsure.summary: Journey visualization showing true multi-touch influence of channels like LinkedIn
best_about_revsure.quote: This is exactly the view I was looking for. it is a key influencer in a in a multi-touch model but always tough to you and you even quantify the number okay.
best_about_revsure.speaker: Adrianna Burrows

best_about_revsure.summary: Out-of-box views that would take weeks for analytics teams to build
best_about_revsure.quote: the kudos we get from analytics teams is, you know, you guys spend weeks building some of our views and even ongoing maintenance. We just present it out of the box to your team
best_about_revsure.speaker: E.T. Mac

best_about_revsure.summary: Exactly what was looking to find out of the tools - marketing effectiveness and multi-touch attribution
best_about_revsure.quote: I love this. This is great. This is exactly like what I was looking to find out of the tools
best_about_revsure.speaker: Inna Malishkevich

best_about_revsure.summary: Platform provides extreme customizable capabilities for attribution timeframe and lookback windows
best_about_revsure.quote: This also provides extreme customizable capabilities, right? So you can toggle between when the lead or off was created, what is the timeframe that you are looking for a campaign to have an engagement with, what is the timeframe that you want to look at spend impression numbers, touch my selection by engagement date, and you can also tweak your look back window for attribution.
best_about_revsure.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

best_about_revsure.summary: Customer journey timeline visualization shows granular touchpoint detail
best_about_revsure.quote: you can go into the individual customer journey and look at like um you know what channels are in the path like for example in this case um you can look at like a timeline what are all the
best_about_revsure.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

immediate.win: Multi-touch attribution replacing last-touch to show true channel value
immediate.quote: the attribution piece is definitely something I'm like really keen on. You know, there's also the spend reallocation, I think those are like two things that are kind of like really near and obvious to me.
immediate.speaker: Theo Stewart

immediate.quote: what Rev sure brings in that's really special about us is we actually tell you if you should stop, wait and watch or increase, right? So this is our predictive AI and our machine learning being able to actually tell you like you should actually increase or you should stop.
immediate.speaker: Alex Cox

immediate.win: Funnel health visibility with stage-to-stage conversion tracking
immediate.quote: I think Funnel Health is another one. Like it's that visibility into, you know, where things are along there in stages. Like we have, we have so much gaps and be able to see certain things easily around our Funnel
immediate.speaker: Joanne Kernie

immediate.win: MQL, pipeline, SQL, closed one, and deals all matching within less than 1% in validation
immediate.quote: I was able to use those to validate MQL, pipeline, SQL, closed one, and deals. So that's all matching within like less than 1% now
immediate.speaker: Theo Stewart

immediate.win: Paid media spend automatically captured and connected for ROI analysis
immediate.quote: how rev shore collects those spend from paid media but everything else is it's everyone's spreadsheet
immediate.speaker: Juyeon Yun

immediate.win: Smart folder structure created to organize 76 views into manageable groups
immediate.quote: these are folders. We can add new folders to this, I believe. Correct me if I'm wrong, Francisco. That makes it easier for you to group views based on use cases and people.
immediate.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

long_term.win: Predictive pipeline coverage by segment replacing heuristic multipliers
long_term.quote: we throw out pipeline coverage model on numbers, multipliers that I think are just heuristics, you know, nothing more. So I think there's some very interesting things there as we go to segments.
long_term.speaker: Kael Kelly

long_term.win: Cross-sell pipeline visibility for existing customers
long_term.quote: I'd love to see, so there's all the points to slice that. I'd love to see this built for cross sale, you know, pipeline. Do we have enough, you know, based on a target, do we have enough pipeline to hit our targets? What stage are these cross-sale opportunities in?
long_term.speaker: Joanne Kernie

long_term.win: Shift digital spend smarter based on multi-touch attribution - optimize paid search vs social vs display allocation
long_term.quote: the really the multi-touch attribution is like to be able to say hey like at the end of the day we're investing i'm making this up ten dollars in paid search five dollars in social and four dollars in display like should we shift up that digital spend in a smarter way
long_term.speaker: Inna Malishkevich

long_term.win: Pipeline and booking predictions using ML models trained exclusively on Avalara data
long_term.quote: we can do SQL, how many SQLs are going to convert, how many dollar value will it generate, and how many, let's say, deals will move into closed ones
long_term.speaker: Shantanu

long_term.win: Replace Excel-based marketing spend tracking with unified platform ROI
long_term.quote: Finance track in Excel has to reach out to certain marketing channel owners and event owners and things like that. So it's everywhere in Excel.
long_term.speaker: Juyeon Yun

long_term.win: Pipeline predictability to inform marketing budget allocation decisions
long_term.quote: marketing wants to know should we need to put more money into top of funnel or not based on pipeline predictability
long_term.speaker: Juyeon Yun

long_term.win: Write-back of marketing influence metrics to Snowflake for executive dashboards
long_term.quote: marketing influence dollar amount marketing influence volume and those there will be new that refs are creating that we should be able to capture in our system
long_term.speaker: Juyeon Yun

before_state.pain_summary: Last-touch attribution in Salesforce misses full buyer journey; manual Snowflake reporting requires hundreds of lines of code; no predictive capability; cannot track net new vs existing customers; orphaned opportunities when contacts change; CFOs cut influencer channels due to attribution blind spots

after_state.with_revsure: AI-powered full-funnel attribution with multi-touch models, predictive pipeline generation/coverage by segment, real-time campaign reallocation recommendations, and unified journey visualization across marketing/SDR/sales touches
wins_realized: Unified funnel report matching Power BI
85% of prospect journeys have marketing touchpoints
Q2 pipeline projections before internal systems
smart folder structure for view organization
open_issues: Adobe Analytics integration pending
Allocadia integration not yet built
requires dedicated headcount (Theo full-time + data modeling support)
Salesforce field history gaps need 6-12 sprints of IT work
daily sync limitation vs real-time for rapid field changes
no pilot/POC without full contract
Partner referral attribution logic mismatch with internal model
Campaign name matching only ~50% accurate
Contact/campaign/lead/account counts not yet validated
Write-back to Snowflake not yet enabled
Custom attribution model development deferred
Data validation standard too strict (3-4% variance)
Custom SQL dependency for funnel metrics
Attribution channel business rules vs MTA model independence
UI too complex for broad rollout
International and customer data not fully ingested
BigQuery access not available for deep analysis
sales activity attribution rules pending
funnel configuration blocked by missing target columns
stage zero pipeline widget incomplete
customer data definitions deferred to Q1
account name sync issue between SFDC and Snowflake
